title: 9 Electronic Bill Payment Reports
body_text:
9	Electronic Bill Payment Reports
9.1	Overview of Electronic Bill Payment Reports
Central 1 produces numerous bill payment reports that are available to all financial institutions that participate in the Electronic Bill Payments Service. See the following table for a summary of these reports and to determine which reports are available to the financial institution.
Report Name
Description
Produced
Distributed To
BPCV
â Bill Payments Tracing Report by Credit Union Branch and Vendor
Lists bill payments that were accepted at Central 1. Sorted by transit number, by biller.
Daily
All participating financial institutions.
BPFC
â Bill Payments, Cancelled Future-Dated Transactions
Lists cancelled future-dated bill payments.
Daily
Financial institutions for which Central 1 holds the bill payments database.
BPMU
â Bill Payments, Daily Maintenance Update
Lists updates to customersâ biller account information.
Daily
Financial institutions for which Central 1 holds the bill payments database.
BPRE
â Bill Payments, Remitted
Lists bill payments that were successfully accepted at Central 1. Sorted by customer number.
Daily
All participating financial institutions.
BPRF
â Bill Payments, Future-Dated Rejected Transactions
Lists rejected future-dated transactions.
Daily
Financial institutions for which Central 1 holds the bill payments database.
BPRS
â Bill Payments, Remitted Summary
Lists the total value of all the financial institutionâs bill payments that were successfully accepted at Central 1.
Daily
All participating financial institutions.
BPRT
â Bill Payments, Rejected Transaction
Lists bill payments that Central 1 rejected or reversed.
Daily
All participating financial institutions.
BPTP
â Bill Payments, 15:00 (Pacific) Billers
Lists bill payments made to billers with a 15:00 (PT) cut-off time.
Daily
All participating financial institutions in Ontario.
BPTR
â Bill Payments, Remitted by Trace
Lists bill payments that were successfully accepted at Central 1 in trace number order.
Daily
All participating financial institutions.
CCMC
â Corporate Client Monthly Tier Charge
Lists corporate bill payment volumes and charges by tier.
Monthly
All participating financial institutions.
ERMC
â
Bill Payment Remittance Processing Monthly Charge
Lists the types of bill payment remitted volumes and amounts.
Monthly
All participating financial institutions.
TPMC
â Tiered Payments Monthly Charge
Lists bill payment volume and charges by tier/rate, and YTD volumes.
Monthly
All participating financial institutions.
TPRE
â
Bill Payments â In-Branch Tax Remitted
Lists tax payments made in-branch.
Daily
All participating financial institutions.
9.2	Accessing Electronic Bill Payment Reports
Electronic bill payment reports are available to participating financial institutions via Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. The BILL file (for all financial institutions except Ontario credit unions) or the ONBL file (for Ontario credit unions) is generally available in the root directory for download the following morning after bill payment transactions have been made.
If applicable, PDFs of the bill payment reports are available in the Bill_Pmt folder. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on accessing Central 1âs File and Report Exchange.
9.3	BPCV â Bill Payments Tracing Report by Credit Union Branch and Vendor
Description
The BPCV provides a detailed list of bill payments accepted at Central 1. This report contains the same information as the BPRE, but it is sorted by biller.
Purpose
Use this report to identify bill payments accepted at Central 1.
Organization
One page is provided per transit number, per biller and one line is provided per transaction. See Figure 1.
Distribution
The report is available the business day after the transaction date and is distributed to all participating financial institutions. A report is produced for each transit number that originated bill payments. A report is only provided if transactions were successfully accepted at Central 1 and only for billers for which payments were received.
Figure 1: BPCV Report
9.4	BPFC â Bill Payments Cancelled Future-Dated Transactions
Description
The BPFC lists cancelled future-dated bill payments by transit number.
Purpose
Financial institutions use the BPFC to identify cancelled future-dated bill payments.
Organization
One page is provided per transit number and one line is provided per transaction. See Figure 2.
Distribution
The report is available the business day after the transaction date and is distributed to financial institutions for which Central 1 holds the bill payments database. A report is produced for each transit number for which cancelled future-dated bill payments were received. A report is only provided if future-dated transactions were cancelled.
Figure 2: BPFC
9.5	BPMU â Bill Payments Daily Maintenance Update
Description
The BPMU lists updates to customersâ biller account information if Central 1 holds the bill payments database.
Purpose
Use this report to identify changes that were made to the customersâ biller account information (e.g., addition or deletion of billers).
Organization
One page is provided per transit number and one line is provided per change. See Figure 3.
Distribution
The report is available the business day after the update is made and is only distributed to financial institutions for which Central 1 holds the bill payments database. A report is produced for each transit number for which changes to customersâ biller account information were received. A report is only provided if Central 1 received such changes.
Figure 3: BPMU
9.6	BPRE â Bill Payments Remitted
Description
The BPRE provides a detailed list of bill payments that were successfully accepted at Central 1. The credit union will be debited for the amount provided in the Grand Total section.
Purpose
Financial institutions use the BPRE to determine which bill payments were successfully accepted at Central 1 and to obtain the settlement amount that was posted to their accounts. If the total provided on this report doesnât match the total provided by the banking system reports, investigate the difference. See Chapter 11, Reconciliation of Electronic Bill Payments, for more information.
Organization
One line is provided for each bill payment transaction. Transactions are sorted in customer account number order. Item counts and dollar amounts are provided in the lower section of the report and are separated by bill payments, tax payments, and trace charges. The summary totals on the BPRE are included on the BPRS Report. See Figure 4.
Distribution
The report is available the business day after the transactions are sent to Central 1 and is distributed to all participating financial institutions. The report is produced for each transit number that originated bill payments. A report is only provided if transactions were successfully accepted at Central 1.
Figure 4: BPRE Report
9.7	BPRF â Bill Payments Future-Dated Rejected Transactions
Description
The BPRF lists future-dated transactions that were rejected at Central 1.
Purpose
Use this report to identify future-dated bill payments that were rejected at Central 1 and to investigate the reason for rejection.
Organization
One line is provided for each rejected future-dated bill payment. See Figure 5.
Distribution
The report is available the business day after a future-dated transaction is rejected and is only distributed to financial institutions for which Central 1 holds the bill payments database. The report is produced for each transit number for which future-dated transactions are rejected. A report is only provided if future-dated transactions are rejected.
Figure 5: BPRF Report
9.8	BPRS â Bill Payments Remitted Summary
Description
The BPRS provides the total value of all the financial institutionâs bill payments that were successfully accepted at Central 1. This report summarizes the dollar value and items counts from each BPRE report.
Purpose
Use the BPRS to obtain the item count and dollar amount of all bill payments that were successfully accepted at Central 1 from all transit numbers of the financial institution. Use this report in conjunction with the BPRE Report.
Organization
A one-line summary is provided for each transit number of the financial institution. Item counts and dollar amounts are provided in the lower section of the report and are separated by bill payments, tax payments, and trace charges. See Figure 6.
Distribution
The report is available the business day after the bill payments are received at Central 1 and is distributed to all participating financial institutions. The report is produced for each financial institution that originated bill payments. A report is only provided if transactions were successfully accepted at Central 1.
Figure 6: BPRS Report
9.9	BPRT â Bill Payments Rejected Transactions
Description
The BPRT provides a list of bill payments that Central 1 rejected or reversed.
Purpose
Use the BPRT to identify rejected bill payments and to assist with bill payment reconciliations. Rejected bill payments are excluded from the settlement total posted to the financial institutionâs account. See Chapter 11, Reconciliation of Electronic Bill Payments, for more information.
Use the report to investigate the rejected transaction with the customer. For example, if the biller account number is invalid, ensure that the information is corrected on the Electronic Bill Payments System.
The BPRT is also used to identify reversed bill payments. Transactions listed on this report must be credited to the customersâ accounts because the reversal process only removes the transaction from Central 1âs Electronic Bill Payments System.
Note:	Central 1 can only reverse a bill payment if Central 1 holds the financial institutionâs electronic bill payments database or if the financial institution transmits the payment to Central 1 before the reversal deadline.
Organization
One line is provided for each rejected or reversed bill payment. The total number and dollar value of rejected and reversed items is provided at the bottom of the report. See Figure 7.
Distribution
The BPRT report is available the business day after the bill payments are rejected or reversed and is distributed to all participating financial institutions. The report is produced for each transit number for which bill payments were rejected or reversed. A report is only provided if transactions were rejected or reversed.
Figure 7: BPRT Report
9.10	BPTP â Bill Payments 15:00 (Pacific) Billers
Description
The BPTP provides a detailed list of bill payments that were made to Vancity VISA, Vancity Com. Inv. Bank Visa, and Community VISA after 3:00 pm PT. These bill payments are sent to the biller on the following business day. Settlement is posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account on the business day after the transactions are sent to the biller.
Purpose
Use this report to help balance and reconcile bill payment transactions. The payments listed on the BPTP arenât included on other bill payment reports for the current day (e.g., the BPTR, BPRS). Instead, these payments are reported with bill payments submitted on the following business day.
Organization
One line is provided for each bill payment. Transactions are sorted in trace number order. Item counts and dollar amounts are provided in the lower section of the report. See Figure 8.
Distribution
The report is available the business day after the bill payments are received at Central 1 and is distributed to all participating financial institutions in Ontario. The report is produced for each transit number that originated bill payments. A report is only provided if transactions made to Vancity VISA, Vancity Com. Inv. Bank Visa, and Community VISA were successfully accepted at Central 1 after 3:00 pm PT.
Note:	This report isnât currently available in BC.
Figure 8: BPTP Report
9.11	BPTR â Bill Payments Remitted by Trace
Description
The BPTR provides a detailed list of bill payments that were successfully accepted at Central 1 in trace number order. The information on this report is the same as what is provided on the BPRE.
Purpose
Use this report to review bill payments accepted at Central 1 by trace number.
Organization
One line is provided for each bill payment. Transactions are sorted in trace number order. Item counts and dollar amounts are provided in the lower section of the report and are separated by bill payments, tax payments, and trace charges. See Figure 9.
Distribution
The report is available the business day after the bill payments are received at Central 1 and is distributed to all participating financial institutions. The report is produced for each transit number that originated bill payments. A report is only provided if transactions were successfully accepted at Central 1.
Figure 9: BPTR Report
9.12	CCMC
â
Corporate Client Monthly Tier Charge Report
Description
The CCMC report provides a detailed list of corporate bill payments that were successfully processed by Central 1.
Purpose
Use this report to reconcile the amount of corporate bill payments and their related tier charges.
Organization
One section is provided for each type of bill payment and its related tier. Another section is provided for any photo retrieval services used.  The last section provides the total amount owing for tier charges. See Figure 10.
Distribution
The monthly report is available the first business day of the month and is distributed to all participating financial institutions. The report is produced for each transit number that submitted corporate bill payments or related services. A report is only provided if applicable transactions took place.
Figure 10: CCMC Report
9.13	ERMC â Bill Payment Remittance Processing Monthly Charge Report
Description
The ERMC provides the volume of bill payments that were successfully remitted through Central 1.
Purpose
Use this report to reconcile the processing method of bill payment volumes and total related fee amounts.
Organization
A section is provided for each type of bill payment based on its processing method. The last provided section is provided for any other fees that apply for the month. See Figure 11.
Distribution
The monthly report is available the first business day of the month and is distributed to all participating financial institutions. The report is produced for each transit number that submitted corporate bill payments or related services. A report is only provided if applicable transactions took place.
Figure 11: ERMC Report
9.14	TPMC â Tiered Payments Monthly Charge Report
Description
The TPMC report provides the tier range, the rate applied per tier, the monthly volume and monthly charges as applied by tier/rate, the total monthly volume, the year-to-date (YTD) volumes by tier, and the YTD total volume for the credit union.
Purpose
Use this report to reconcile bill payment charges for the month. The report provides information for year-to-date volume.
Organization
One line is provided for each tier rate. The monthly and YTD totals are provided at the bottom of the applicable columns. The subtotal and the total charges are displayed at the end of the report.
Distribution
The report is available on the second business day of each month and is distributed to all participating financial institutions. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: TPMC Report
9.15 	TPRE â Bill Payments â In-Branch Tax Remitted Report
Description
The TPRE report provides a detailed list of tax payments remitted in-branch.
Purpose
Use this report to reconcile in-branch tax payment amounts and payment totals.
Organization
One line is provided for each customerâs tax payment that is remitted in-branch. The totals are provided at the bottom of the applicable columns. The subtotal and the total charges are displayed at the end of the report.
Distribution
The report is available the next business day after any in-branch tax payments are processed. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: TPRE Report
END
